articles.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
articles.description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

articles.title: Air Canada suspends flights to New York’s JFK airport, citing surging jet fuel costs
articles.description: Airline says it will stop daily flights from Montreal and Toronto to JFK starting June 1

articles.title: Japan reveals new name for 40C-and-hotter days after blistering summer
articles.description: The term - kokushobi - translates to "cruelly hot", "brutally hot" or "severely hot", and comes after Japan's hottest summer on record.

articles.description: The UK government blocked the rapper from entering the country over his history of making pro-Nazi and racist statements.

articles.title: Laval enters intervention mode as rising waters reach minor flood threshold
articles.description: The City of Laval has moved into intervention mode as rapidly rising water levels reach the threshold for minor flooding, though officials say the situation remains under control for now. articles.title: Canada’s emissions reductions slowed in 2024, federal data shows
articles.description: A new report from the federal government says an increase in greenhouse gas emissions from the oil and gas industry in 2024 offset decreases in several other industries.

articles.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
articles.description: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real-life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.

articles.title: Suspected crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
articles.description: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

articles.title: Zoom teams up with World to verify humans in meetings
articles.description: Meeting platform Zoom has announced a partnership with World, Sam Altman's human ID verification company, to ensure that the people attending meetings are actually human and not AI-generated imposters. Zoom will show a badge on verified participants' tile.
